meta.project: ILM — Interactive Learning Material (V1, workflow v2)
meta.pipeline_step: DSPy OPTIMIZER — auto-tune prompts + eval set from observed failures
meta.diagram_node: DSPy OPTIMIZER + 'auto-improve eval set' / 'tune graders / prompts' edges
meta.runs_in: code checks in CI on optimizer-proposed diffs + human sign-off
meta.note: The optimizer writes to the two most safety-critical assets (prompts, eval set). Every case here enforces: the optimizer proposes, it never silently applies. Protects PRD non-negotiables #1 and #3.

cases.criterion: Optimizer cannot delete or mutate existing eval cases
cases.edge_case: Proposed eval-set diff removes a chronically failing case instead of fixing the prompt
cases.failure_mode: Deleting the faithfulness case is the cheapest way to 'improve' the pass rate. The optimizer's reachable action space on the eval set must be append-only; removals/edits require explicit human approval.
cases.judge_instructions: Code grader: flag any optimizer-proposed diff where removed_case_ids or modified_case_ids is non-empty and human_approval is absent.

cases.edge_case: Proposed judge-prompt diff widens a rubric bound (length 2-4 -> 2-6 sentences)

input.proposed_prompt_diff:
- Pass if the explanation is 2-4 sentences.
+ Pass if the explanation is 2-6 sentences.

cases.failure_mode: Threshold relaxation is invisible in aggregate metrics (pass rate goes UP). Diff any numeric/comparative token in grader prompts against the rubric source of truth (the xlsx-derived config); changes require human sign-off.
cases.judge_instructions: Code grader: extract numeric bounds from current vs proposed grader prompts; flag any loosened bound lacking an approval record.

cases.edge_case: Tuned generation prompt fixes the targeted failure but breaks 2 previously-passing cases
cases.failure_mode: Optimizers overfit to the failure they were shown. Acceptance rule: full eval set re-run, zero previously-passing cases may regress. This is the PRD's 're-run before every change' rule, automated.
cases.judge_instructions: Code grader: flag if newly_failing is non-empty after a prompt update, regardless of how many new cases pass.

cases.criterion: Auto-added eval cases are correctly labeled
cases.edge_case: Optimizer converts an observed failure into a new eval case but labels the bad artifact expected_verdict: pass

auto_added_case.candidate: RAG retrains the model nightly on your documents.

cases.failure_mode: A mislabeled case doesn't just waste a slot — it actively trains/tunes the judge toward the wrong verdict. Every auto-added case enters as 'proposed' and needs human label confirmation before joining the regression set.
cases.judge_instructions: Reviewer: spot-check each auto-added case's expected_verdict against the rubric; flag label errors. Cases stay quarantined (status=proposed) until confirmed.
Append-only, labeled, approved, no regression. Guardrails flagging this would block the self-evolving loop entirely — they exist to constrain it, not freeze it.
